entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on proverbs 21:23
entries.excerpt: Pambo said to Antony, 'What shall I do?' Antony said, 'Do not trust in your own righteousness. Do not go on sorrowing over a deed that is past. Keep your tongue and your belly under control.'

entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on proverbs 21:23
entries.excerpt: Gregory said, 'God asks three things of anyone who is baptized: to keep the true faith with all his soul and all his might; to control his tongue; to be chaste in his body.'
